assertions: Guided Start accepted a real PDF upload and disclosed the OCR confidence threshold only after the optical source was selected.
At the default 80% threshold, Audiveris retained all 236 recognized notes for Score-Anchored Review.
After cancellation, changing the visible threshold to 70% and choosing Restart from source created a new OMR run at the 70% policy instead of reusing the stale 80% policy.
The recommended Principal Voice decision advanced the workflow without returning to OCR review.
The baroque-guitar and 13-course-lute targets completed with retained PDF, SVG, LilyPond, MIDI, and playback-mapping deliverables.
The completed baroque-guitar and lute arrangements reopened from persisted workspace URLs and displayed PDF available, preservation audit pass, and literal Audio Preview controls.
Baroque-guitar playback advanced from 0:00 to a concrete occurrence marker and visibly followed the engraved score with highlighted notes and a red play-position line.
A target-search failure was durably recoverable: reopening Guided Start showed Stopped at projection (search_exhausted) and retained the two completed outputs.
The live saved lute SVG rendered its French tablature letters as legible black text on the white score rather than black knockout squares.
New arrangement, Projects, and Knowledge & defaults rendered together in the left chat-column action bar without overlapping the score preview.
knownLimitations: The classical-guitar sibling exhausted the current 10000-state bounded phrase search and did not produce a deliverable in this run. This is an accepted prototype limitation covered by the arrangement-intelligence-followup target-voice and cross-target acceptance slices.
